game_info.title: Timberman Clone
game_info.concept:
Become a Timberman, chop wood and avoid the branches. Almost 30 million players could not be wrong - featured as one of the best casual games. Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/398710/Timberman/ ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ Become a Timberman, chop wood and avoid the branches. Almost 30 million players could not be wrong - featured as one of the best casual games.

Almost 30 million players could not be wrong - featured as one of the best casual games.



Now new Timbermill Mode: 8 Players! And Dark Father as a new timber-hero!





Exclusively on Steam: now you can play multiplayer with friend(s) or random opponent from the Internet!



Timberman is an oldschool arcade style casual game. Become a Timberman, chop wood and avoid the branches. Sounds like an easy task? It's easy to play but hard to master.





4 variable environments and 32 + 5 exclusive Steam Timbermen to unlock. Master your skills for the top records on the leaderboards.



Take your axe as every lumberjack does and chop the tree as fast as you can!
game_info.description:
You are the Master Lumberjack. Your goal is to chop down an infinite tree as fast as you can.
Branches will appear on the left or right side of the tree. You must position yourself on the opposite side to avoid getting hit.
Every chop earns points and replenishes your energy bar. If the energy bar runs out, the game ends.
Be quick, be precise, and avoid the branches!
game_info.controls:
- Left Arrow: Move to the Left side and Chop
- Right Arrow: Move to the Right side and Chop
- Enter: Start Game
- R: Restart Game (from Game Over)
- Esc: Pause / Unpause

TEST_1.test_description: Verify that the automated player can identify branch positions and successfully chop wood without dying.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test controller reads the 'branchSide' of the tree segment directly above the player's head. If the branch is on the Left, the controller inputs 'Right'. If the branch is on the Right, the controller inputs 'Left'. If no branch, it maintains the current side.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should accumulate a score of at least 50 points without triggering the "GAME_OVER_LOSE" state, demonstrating valid collision avoidance logic.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify the fail state mechanics by intentionally colliding with a branch.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The controller identifies the position of the next branch and intentionally moves the player to that side to trigger a collision.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game phase should transition to "GAME_OVER_LOSE" immediately upon the chop action, confirming collision detection works.

TEST_3.strategy_description: The controller will start the game and then perform no inputs (idle).
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The energy bar should deplete over time. Once it hits zero, the game phase should transition to "GAME_OVER_LOSE".
